content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach avoids every priority AoA anti-pattern across the conversation. Most notably, when the client explicitly baits the coach on turn 13 ('we got to hold off on the insights'), the coach responds with a clean 'Okay' (turn 14) — the canonical meet-resistance-with-okay move, declining both loving-to-transform and view-as-technique bait. The coach does not chase breakthrough, does not fix the chronic pain ('I'm sorry. I'm just sorry that that's the case for you' on turn 22 sits with it rather than reframing). When the client says they don't want to appear like a victim, the coach affirms the victim status without shaming the grief process ('You're a victim, bro' on turn 26) — the opposite of validating the resistance. Shame is met with 'how would you enjoy your shame?' (turn 34) rather than fought, argued with, or labeled as a limiting belief. The projection move on turn 30 uses self-disclosure as material, not as evangelism. No question-stacking-as-extraction; questions are warm and point to now. No safety-as-control, no apology extraction, no methodology jargon deployed.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 14, 22, 24, 26, 30, 34, 36, 40],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'How about right now?' — refuses the future-fix frame without lecturing about it. No view-as-technique."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names said-vs-shown without interpretation/diagnosis. No evangelizing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Canonical meet-resistance-with-okay. Declines the bait to defend or push insight. Strong positive avoidance of loving-to-transform."},
    {"turn": 16, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reframes rush as potentially enjoyable rather than as a problem to fix. Avoids fixing-people."},
    {"turn": 22, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Receives pain with 'I'm sorry' — does not coach, reframe, or transform it."},
    {"turn": 24, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Sadness offered as something to enjoy, not to be solved or transformed away. Avoids trying-to-get-rid-of-the-emotion."},
    {"turn": 26, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Affirms victim status — avoids dont-shame-your-grief-process, refuses to validate resistance to identity."},
    {"turn": 30, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Self-disclosure as material, not as evangelism or vulnerability-as-control. Warm, in service of the client."},
    {"turn": 34, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'How would you enjoy your shame?' — does not fight the inner critic; includes it."},
    {"turn": 36, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Paradoxical inversion surfaces the mechanism without forcing change."},
    {"turn": 40, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Frames as experiment — client finds out. Avoids prescriptive fixing."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["frame-it-as-an-experiment", "calibrate-the-threshold", "shame-as-disconnection", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "are-you-bracing-or-embracing"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Textbook avoidance — the turn-14 'Okay' is the signature move and would be the single most likely place for an AI coach to trip into defending or pushing."
}
